theme: district boiler fleet meets a winter heat demand
title: Sweet-Spot Boiler Fleet: Co-Optimized Commitment and Loading for Winter Heat
strategies: trivial: every boiler online for the whole horizon, demand split proportional to capacity (raised to pmin) -- reproduces the checker reference, wastes no-load fuel and never sits on the sweet spot

greedy priority list: commit once a cheapest static prefix that covers PEAK demand, keep it online all horizon, split demand proportionally -- saves the no-load of unlit boilers but runs the committed fleet cold at the shoulders and loads it off the sweet spot

strong: co-optimize commitment and loading -- size the online fleet so it sits near its ~70% sweet spot at the CURRENT demand (fewer boilers at the shoulders, more at the peak), light/de-light along the demand ramp so ramp limits are met for free, and dispatch by equal marginal cost
co-optimization with anticipation: pre-position boilers before a sharp demand ramp and hold a boiler online through a short trough when its min-down time would forbid a timely restart
